food_subgroup: Yogurt-based curry with deep-fried gram flour fritters

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for Kadhi Pakora. Mapped to broader 'curry dish' class (FOODON_03311737) as the closest defensible ancestor. A more specific term for yogurt-besan curry with fritters does not exist in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Curry, meat or vegetable based, cooked dish — used as closest FoodEx2 analogue for a spiced yogurt-gram flour curry with fried fritters
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Kadhi Pakora. A0FJP (curry-based cooked dish) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. No exact match exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Kadhi Pakora or equivalent yogurt-besan curry dish as of 2024.

allergens: gluten (gram flour may be cross-contaminated; besan itself is gluten-free but processing risk exists)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving of Kadhi Pakora is approximately 250 g, typically served in a bowl alongside steamed rice or roti at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Kadhi Pakora is not listed as a single composite entry in ICMR-NIN tables. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN component data for yogurt (dahi), gram flour (besan), onion, ghee, and spices, weighted to a representative recipe formulation. Energy cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients. Saturated fat estimated from ghee and yogurt fat fractions per ICMR-NIN and USDA data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kadhi Pakora as a composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the dominant ingredients: gram flour (besan) GI ~28–35 per University of Sydney GI Database, and full-fat yogurt GI ~36 per Atkinson et al. 2008. The presence of protein (4.8 g/100g), fat (9.2 g/100g), and dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) further attenuates glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: (45 × 10.5 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.8 g. Confidence: 0.7 (estimated).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (320 mg/100g; ~800 mg per 250 g serving) from added salt and spice blends, combined with saturated fat from ghee and deep-frying oil
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150 g or less, request reduced salt preparation, substitute ghee with a small amount of unsaturated oil, and consider air-frying or baking the pakoras to reduce both sodium and saturated fat load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) due to gram flour and yogurt base, but moderate carbohydrate content (10.5 g/100g) and deep-fried pakoras increase caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kadhi Pakora is acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in controlled portions (150–200 g), paired with non-starchy vegetables rather than rice; the low GI of besan and yogurt supports a blunted postprandial glucose response, but pakora quantity should be limited to reduce fat and calorie intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (~3.8 g/100g estimated) and deep-frying oil in pakoras raises LDL-cholesterol risk; however, gram flour provides soluble fibre and plant protein which are cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, Kadhi Pakora should be prepared with minimal ghee (or replaced with a small amount of olive or canola oil), pakoras should be baked or air-fried, and sodium should be reduced; the gram flour and yogurt base itself is cardioprotective when prepared with low fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Turmeric and fenugreek seeds have anti-inflammatory properties; however, deep-fried pakoras may contribute to pro-inflammatory dietary patterns
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, fenugreek) in the kadhi base, but should limit deep-fried pakora intake and prefer baked or air-fried versions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kadhi Pakora is a classic North Indian comfort dish, deeply rooted in Punjabi and Rajasthani culinary traditions. The dish consists of two components: a tangy, golden-yellow yogurt-based gravy (kadhi) and deep-fried gram flour fritters (pakoras). The kadhi is prepared by whisking sour yogurt (dahi) with gram flour (besan) and water to form a smooth emulsion, which is then slow-simmered with turmeric, salt, and spices until it thickens to a silky, slightly viscous consistency. The pakoras are made by coating sliced onions or spinach leaves in a spiced besan batter and deep-frying them until crisp and golden. The fritters are added to the simmering kadhi and allowed to absorb the gravy. The dish is finished with a hot tempering (tadka) of ghee, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds (methi), mustard seeds, and whole dried red chilies, which is poured over the kadhi to release a deeply aromatic, smoky fragrance. The flavour profile is tangy, mildly spiced, and rich, with a contrast between the soft, gravy-soaked pakoras and the creamy kadhi. Nutritionally, per 100 g, Kadhi Pakora provides approximately 142 kcal, 4.8 g protein, 9.2 g fat, and 10.5 g carbohydrates, with 110 mg calcium and 1.8 g dietary fibre. The gram flour base contributes plant protein and soluble fibre, while yogurt provides calcium, probiotics, and riboflavin. The dish has a low glycemic index (~45) due to the besan and yogurt combination, making it moderately suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. However, its sodium content (~320 mg/100g) and saturated fat from ghee and deep-frying require attention for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. It is vegetarian, halal-compatible, and a staple of traditional North Indian family meals, particularly during the monsoon season.

confidence_reason: Kadhi Pakora is not listed as a single composite entry in ICMR-NIN published tables. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN component data for yogurt, gram flour, ghee, onion, and spices, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy ingredient data. Saturated fat is estimated from ghee and yogurt fat fractions. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for gram flour and yogurt. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) is estimated from the ghee and yogurt fat fractions using ICMR-NIN and USDA SR Legacy data; no direct composite measurement was available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile and moisture data for this traditional composite preparation are not available in ICMR-NIN published tables or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Kadhi Pakora as a composite dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2024. 4. The input record contained four duplicate portion entries (all '1 serving 250g'); deduplicated to a single default serving of 250 g. 5. The input alias language code 'ra' (Rajasthani) is not a standard ISO 639-1 code; Rajasthani is typically represented as 'raj' (ISO 639-3). The alias कढ़ी has been retained under Hindi (hi) as it is the same script and closely related form. 6. Dietary tag 'low_calorie' from the input has been removed as 142 kcal/100g and ~355 kcal per 250 g serving does not meet standard low-calorie thresholds. 7. All core macros (protein_g 4.8, fat_g 9.2, carbohydrates_g 10.5) are non-zero and sourced from ICMR-NIN adapted data; no zero-macro justification is required. 8. ontology.foodex2_match_type corrected from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the permitted enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by G5.
